advantages: Native interactivity—zoom, pan, legend toggle, and hover—without custom JavaScript in notebooks.
Plotly Express mirrors DataFrame workflows learners already know from pandas and Seaborn, lowering the ramp-up for interactive EDA.
write_html produces self-contained shareable artifacts for ML experiment review outside Jupyter.
Graph Objects and make_subplots support multi-metric training dashboards with aligned epoch axes and unified hover.
disadvantages: Larger HTML payloads and browser memory use than static Matplotlib PNGs—very large scatter plots (millions of points) need downsampling or WebGL (`scattergl`).
Static publication workflows still favor Matplotlib/Seaborn savefig; Plotly raster export requires Kaleido and loses interactivity.
Dash dashboards add deployment and callback complexity beyond one-off notebook figures.
Default styling can feel web-app generic until layout, template, and color sequences are tuned for ML reports.
